name: Supreme Court of India
description: The highest judicial authority in India. Hears appeals from High Courts and original suits involving disputes between the Union and States or fundamental rights violations.

name: High Courts
description: The principal civil courts of original jurisdiction in the state along with appellate jurisdiction over all subordinate courts.

name: District & Sessions Courts
description: District Courts handle civil matters, while Sessions Courts handle criminal matters. Appeals lie to the High Court.

name: Subordinate Courts (Civil Judge / Magistrate)
description: The lowest tier of the formal judiciary handling smaller civil disputes and lesser criminal offenses.

name: Specialized Tribunals
description: Quasi-judicial bodies for specific areas like Consumer Disputes (NCDRC), Company Law (NCLT), Armed Forces (AFT), and Labour matters.
